concerns.text: Task T005 implements 'missing mask... detection' but the spec Edge Cases mandate a 'no-op' safe failure mode for missing masks. The plan explicitly rejects this 'no-op' in favor of aborting with an error (Constitution Principle II). T005 silently weakens the plan's constitutional requirement to abort, reverting to the spec's weaker 'no-op' fallback.

Task T014 integrates 'author-kit' inference. The plan mandates 'Real-Call Testing' (Constitution Principle III) on real sample data. T014 does not explicitly mandate the use of real data or forbid mocks/stubs in the integration step, creating a risk of silent fallback to synthetic data which violates the plan's methodology.

Tasks T022/T023 implement 'Relative Speedup' and 'Relative Stability'. The spec SC-002/SC-003 mandate absolute thresholds (12.66 FPS, 30dB). The plan explicitly flags these SCs as 'BLOCKED' and implements relative metrics instead. T022/T023 do not include a verification step to explicitly log the 'Spec Conflict' or 'BLOCKED' status for SC-002/SC-003, risking a silent acceptance of the relative metric as a pass for the absolute SC.

Task T033 validates 'background stability against mask regions (FR-004)'. FR-004 requires 'no visible flickering'. The plan defines the metric as 'Flicker Score < 3x Noise Floor'. T033 lacks the specific quantitative pass/fail criterion (3x Noise Floor) in its description, relying on the undefined FR-004. This creates a semantic gap where the task's verification step is not fully defined by the spec's acceptance criteria.

Task T025 implements 'Chunk Boundary Score'. The plan identifies this as a 'Missing SC' (Spec-Root Cause) and flags it as 'BLOCKED' in the final report. T025 implements the metric but lacks a corresponding task to 'Report as FAIL (Spec Gap)' or explicitly document the missing SC in the final output, failing to preserve the plan's requirement to flag this gap.
